title: SPARTA Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Overlap/Correlation Method v1
research_objective: Pin the EXACT method, inputs, units, range, and validation rules for measuring overlap, correlation, redundancy, and independence between any two FROZEN admissible strategies -- the rho_ij and redundancy signals consumed by the P2 metrics (diversification_ratio, marginal_capital_efficiency) and the P3 baselines (equal_risk refinement, marginal-efficiency ranking). This bundle is spec/framework-only: it defines HOW each measure WOULD be computed from FROZEN per-trade / per-period series; it computes NO real overlap, NO real correlation, and produces NO real portfolio result. Computation is a separate, separately-authorized bundle.

verdict_ceiling_rules.rationale: Downstream, advisory overlap/correlation measures inherit the upstream registries' WATCH ceiling and must never imply readiness not earned.

c10_boundary.note: This method does not read, connect to, or depend on Candidate #10 indicator results, replay, or working-tree state. C10's per-trade / per-period series are exactly the shape these measures consume, but C10 is DEFERRED: it becomes eligible only once it has its own FROZEN, committed replay artifact admitted under the P1 Input Contract. Never the working tree.

required_frozen_series.note: These measures need richer FROZEN per-trade / per-period series than the P1 summary fields -- supplied by a committed, checksummed replay artifact (admissible only when frozen + committed). A future P1 amendment or the frozen replay artifact itself admits these series. No live strategy results are ever read.

series.desc: Per-bar boolean: strategy i in a position at time t (from frozen replay).
Per-bar capital-at-risk of strategy i at time t (from frozen replay).
Per-trade / per-bar symbol exposure (from frozen replay).
Per-trade / per-bar direction: long / short / flat (from frozen replay).
Per-period (e.g., daily) return stream of strategy i (from frozen replay).
Per-period boolean: strategy i in a drawdown window at time t (from frozen replay).

shared_symbols.i_j: Two distinct FROZEN admissible strategies being compared.
shared_symbols.T_common: Aligned common time index over which both strategies have frozen series (intersection of their frozen windows).
shared_symbols.N_min: Pre-declared minimum aligned-sample count required before any correlation/overlap is defined (operator constant; never inferred).
shared_symbols.rho_ij: Pearson correlation of the two frozen period-return streams over T_common; the value consumed by P2 + P3.
shared_symbols.tau_low: Pre-declared low redundancy threshold for the independent class (operator constant).
shared_symbols.tau_high: Pre-declared high redundancy threshold for the redundant class (operator constant).
shared_symbols.rho_low: Pre-declared low absolute-correlation threshold for the independent class (operator constant).

methods.label: Trade-time overlap percentage
methods.formula: overlap_time(i,j) = |{ t in T_common : in_position_i(t) AND in_position_j(t) }| / |{ t in T_common : in_position_i(t) OR in_position_j(t) }|  (Jaccard over in-position time).
methods.range: [0, 1]; 1 = always in a position at the same time.
validation_rules: Computed only over the aligned T_common.
If the union (either-in-position) set is empty, the measure is UNDEFINED and flagged, never 0.
Inputs are FROZEN per-bar in-position flags from a committed replay artifact.

methods.label: Simultaneous capital-at-risk overlap
methods.formula: car_overlap(i,j) = ( sum_{t in T_common} min(car_i(t), car_j(t)) ) / ( sum_{t in T_common} max(car_i(t), car_j(t)) ).
validation_rules: Computed only over the aligned T_common.
If sum of max(car) over T_common is 0, the measure is UNDEFINED and flagged, never 0.
Inputs are FROZEN per-bar capital-at-risk series from a committed replay artifact.

methods.label: Same-symbol overlap
methods.formula: same_symbol(i,j) = |{ t in T_common : in_position_i(t) AND in_position_j(t) AND symbol_i(t) == symbol_j(t) }| / |{ t in T_common : in_position_i(t) AND in_position_j(t) }|.
methods.units: fraction of simultaneous in-position time on the same symbol, dimensionless.
validation_rules: Denominator is the simultaneous in-position time; if it is empty, UNDEFINED and flagged.
Symbols use the SPARTA canonical mapping; an unmapped symbol is flagged, never silently matched.
Inputs are FROZEN per-bar symbol exposure from a committed replay artifact.

methods.label: Same-direction overlap
methods.formula: same_dir(i,j) = |{ t : simultaneous same-symbol AND direction_i(t) == direction_j(t) }| / |{ t : simultaneous same-symbol }|. Opposite-direction simultaneous same-symbol exposure is reported separately as a hedging_offset flag.
methods.units: fraction of simultaneous same-symbol time in the same direction, dimensionless.
methods.range: [0, 1]; opposite direction (hedging) flagged separately, never folded silently.
validation_rules: Denominator is the simultaneous same-symbol time; if empty, UNDEFINED and flagged.
Opposite-direction overlap is surfaced as a hedging_offset flag, not netted into the score.
Inputs are FROZEN per-bar direction from a committed replay artifact.

methods.label: Return-stream correlation (rho_ij)
methods.formula: rho_ij = cov(r_i, r_j) / ( sd(r_i) * sd(r_j) ) computed over T_common with at least N_min aligned periods. This rho_ij is the value consumed by P2 (diversification_ratio, marginal_capital_efficiency) and P3 (equal_risk refinement, ranking).
validation_rules: Computed only over the aligned T_common with at least N_min periods; otherwise UNDEFINED and flagged.
If sd(r_i) == 0 or sd(r_j) == 0, rho_ij is UNDEFINED and flagged, never set to 0.
No lookahead: returns are the frozen per-period series; windows are not re-fit.
Inputs are FROZEN per-period return streams from a committed replay artifact.

methods.label: Drawdown-period overlap
methods.formula: dd_overlap(i,j) = |{ t in T_common : drawdown_i(t) AND drawdown_j(t) }| / |{ t in T_common : drawdown_i(t) OR drawdown_j(t) }|  (Jaccard over drawdown-period time).
methods.range: [0, 1]; 1 = strategies bleed in the same windows.
validation_rules: Computed only over the aligned T_common.
If neither strategy is ever in drawdown over T_common, the measure is UNDEFINED and flagged, never 0.
Inputs are FROZEN per-period drawdown flags from a committed replay artifact.

methods.label: Redundancy score
methods.formula: redundancy(i,j) = ( w_tt*trade_time_overlap + w_car*car_overlap + w_sym*same_symbol + w_dir*same_dir + w_rho*max(rho_ij, 0) + w_dd*dd_overlap ) / ( w_tt + w_car + w_sym + w_dir + w_rho + w_dd ). Component weights are pre-declared operator constants. Only the positive part of rho_ij contributes to redundancy (negative correlation is diversifying, not redundant).
validation_rules: Component weights are pre-declared constants; never fit to data.
Any UNDEFINED component is EXCLUDED from the weighted average (weights renormalised over defined components); if ALL components are UNDEFINED, redundancy is UNDEFINED.
Only max(rho_ij, 0) feeds redundancy; negative correlation routes to diversification, not redundancy.

methods.label: Diversification benefit score
methods.formula: diversification_benefit(i,j) = 1 - redundancy(i,j). Reported alongside the P2 diversification_ratio for the full portfolio; higher = adding j given i is more diversifying.
validation_rules: If redundancy(i,j) is UNDEFINED, diversification_benefit is UNDEFINED, never defaulted to 1.
This is an advisory signal, not a profit estimate.

classes.rule: redundancy(i,j) is UNDEFINED, or rho_ij is UNDEFINED where the independent test needs it, or fewer than N_min aligned periods. NEVER silently coerced to another class.

validation_rules: tau_low < tau_high; both in [0, 1]; rho_low in [0, 1]; N_min a positive integer. All pre-declared; never fit.
The 'undefined' class is mandatory: insufficient data or an UNDEFINED redundancy/correlation classifies as undefined, never as independent or redundant.
Classification inherits the WATCH ceiling; it never surfaces PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG.

downstream_consumers.P2_metric_spec: rho_ij feeds diversification_ratio + marginal_capital_efficiency; redundancy/overlap inform candidate_overlap context.
downstream_consumers.P3_allocation_baseline_spec: rho_ij + redundancy inform equal_risk refinements and the ranking_only_marginal_efficiency ordering.
downstream_consumers.note: These are spec dependencies only; no value is computed or passed in this bundle.

method_validation_rules.frozen_admissible_inputs_only: Every input is a FROZEN per-trade / per-period series from a committed, checksummed replay artifact admissible under P1; live strategy results, networked data, and uncommitted working trees are inadmissible.
method_validation_rules.aligned_common_window_required: Every pairwise measure is computed only over the aligned T_common; mismatched windows are never zero-padded or forward-filled.
method_validation_rules.minimum_sample_size: No correlation/overlap is defined on fewer than N_min aligned periods; below N_min the measure (and any dependent classification) is UNDEFINED.
method_validation_rules.no_lookahead: Series are used as frozen; no window is re-fit and no future information leaks across the decision time.
method_validation_rules.reproducibility: Same frozen series + same pre-declared constants reproduce the same measures bit-for-bit on a second offline run.
method_validation_rules.pre_declared_constants_never_fit: Component weights, tau_low, tau_high, rho_low, and N_min are operator-declared constants and are NEVER fit, tuned, or inferred from data.
method_validation_rules.undefined_over_silent_default: Any empty-set, zero-variance, or insufficient-sample case yields an explicit UNDEFINED+flag, never a silent 0 / 1 / +inf.
method_validation_rules.negative_correlation_is_diversifying: Negative return correlation reduces redundancy and increases diversification benefit; it is never treated as overlap.
method_validation_rules.no_real_overlap_or_correlation_in_this_b: This bundle computes NO real overlap and NO real correlation; it is spec-only.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: A future overlap/correlation computation is PASS-grade FOR REVIEW only when: every input is a FROZEN admissible per-trade/per-period series (P1); measures use the aligned T_common with at least N_min periods; pre-declared constants are operator-supplied; UNDEFINED handling is respected; the independence class is one of {independent, partial_overlap, redundant, undefined}; outputs respect the WATCH ceiling; and the run reproduces bit-for-bit. PASS means 'measures fit for human review', never 'allocate' or 'execute'.
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Measures computable but borderline (short aligned window near N_min, several UNDEFINED components accepted by the operator, sparse drawdown overlap); documented and re-checked.
pass_watch_fail_rules.FAIL: Any input is inadmissible/unfrozen/live; a window was zero-padded or forward-filled; a pre-declared constant was fit to data; a zero-variance correlation was silently set to 0; an UNDEFINED case was coerced to a definite class; or the WATCH ceiling was bypassed.

required_future_artifacts: Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Advisory Memo Schema v1 (P5) -- finalises the advisory report schema that hosts these overlap/correlation measures + the independence classification; spec only.
A possible P1 Input Contract amendment that formally admits the FROZEN per-trade / per-period series these measures require; spec only.
A separately-authorized COMPUTE bundle that actually runs these measures over frozen admissible series and emits rho_ij + redundancy + classifications.
Lane closeout memo if the capital-efficiency idea PARKs / RETIREs.
safety_boundaries: Research-only. Advisory-only. Overlap/correlation method spec / framework only.
No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No live capital allocation. No paper-order execution. No order placement. No order sizing for execution.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this method's runtime.
No real overlap or correlation computed in this bundle. No real portfolio result produced. No data fetch, no detector, no replay, no backtest.
Do not read live strategy results. Do not read, edit, stage, or modify Candidate #10 files, tests, artifacts, labels, replay, detector, or working-tree state.
Candidate #10 remains deferred and is not connected by this method; C10 must finish and freeze by itself first.
Consume only FROZEN, committed, admissible artifacts; never live positions or uncommitted working trees.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this method alone.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns. An advisory allocation is not an execution instruction. No allocation is authorized by this method alone.
no_profit_claim_policy: This method does not imply edge and does not allocate capital.
A defined overlap or correlation measure does not imply profit.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns.
An advisory allocation memo is a human-review aid, never an execution instruction.
No allocation is authorized by this method alone (a separate compute bundle requires its own authorization).
